content:
VERDICT: CORRECT

REASONING:
1. **Dimensional consistency**: All quantities involved are dimensionless.  
   - \( D_{\text{inf}} = e^{-3N} \times (T_{\text{reh}}/M_{\text{GUT}})^{3/2} \) is a product of dimensionless factors.  
   - \( \Xi = [(\alpha/M) M_{\text{Pl}}] \times D_{\text{inf}} \) multiplies two dimensionless numbers, so \( \Xi \) is dimensionless, matching its role in \( \rho_\Lambda = \Xi M_{\text{Pl}}^4 \).

2. **Numerical computations**:  
   - Given \( N = 92 \), \( 3N = 276 \), so \( e^{-3N} = e^{-276} \).  
   - \( T_{\text{reh}} = 10^{15} \, \text{GeV} \), \( M_{\text{GUT}} = 10^{16} \, \text{GeV} \), so \( T_{\text{reh}}/M_{\text{GUT}} = 10^{-1} \), and \( (10^{-1})^{3/2} = 10^{-1.5} \).  
   - \( \log_{10}(e^{-276}) = -276 \log_{10}(e) \approx -276 \times 0.4343 = -119.87 \), hence \( e^{-276} \approx 10^{-119.87} \).  
   - Then \( D_{\text{inf}} \approx 10^{-119.87} \times 10^{-1.5} = 10^{-121.37} \approx 10^{-121} \) (order-of-magnitude rounding is acceptable, as \( 10^{-121.37} \) lies within the range \( 10^{-121.5} \) to \( 10^{-120.5} \)).  
   - With \( (\alpha/M) M_{\text{Pl}} \sim 10^{-2} \), \( \Xi \sim 10^{-2} \times 10^{-121} = 10^{-123} \).

3. **Comparison to observed cosmological constant**:  
   - \( \rho_\Lambda \sim (2.3 \, \text{meV})^4 = (2.3 \times 10^{-3} \, \text{eV})^4 \approx 2.8 \times 10^{-11} \, \text{eV}^4 \).  
   - \( M_{\text{Pl}} \sim 1.22 \times 10^{28} \, \text{eV} \), so \( M_{\text{Pl}}^4 \approx 2.2 \times 10^{112} \, \text{eV}^4 \).  
   - \( \rho_\Lambda / M_{\text{Pl}}^4 \approx 1.26 \times 10^{-123} \sim 10^{-123} \), matching the computed \( \Xi \).

No sign errors, index violations, or logical gaps are present in the given steps. The approximations are justified for an order-of-magnitude estimate.

ERRORS: None.

SEVERITY: N/A
